question: Does the CK direct-fragment dKV producer win survive real TorchTitan LightningIndexer routes, or was it only a clustered synthetic proxy result?

code_changes.change: Added --kernel-backend ck to feed real actual-LI materialized probs/grad_logits into probe_compressed_dkv_ck_route_grid_raw_atomic.py. CK backend supports raw_atomic and contrib_sink only.

memory.read: Peak includes diagnostic materialized P/scaled-dP and route metadata. This is why the result is not an e2e promotion even though the kernel slice is fast.

route_model.read: Real LI route density is lower than full synthetic topk because about 0.525M selected slots are invalid/zeroed. The CK direct-fragment raw atomic path is already fast enough that local dKV write reduction is not the next isolated wall unless it is fused with source-boundary recompute.

decision.promote: Use CK direct-fragment MFMA as the dKV contribution owner for the next source-boundary sparse-MLA backward rewrite.
decision.demote: Do not spend another branch on late dKV-only reducers that still consume materialized P/scaled-dP; the actual-LI CK raw dKV slice is 5.66 ms, while the bridge/materialization problem remains outside this slice.
decision.next_kernel_contract: Move CK direct-fragment contribution math into the no-HSK source-boundary owner: q,dO,kv,indices,lse/output -> recompute QK/probs/scaled-dP inside the kernel -> produce dQ and dKV contributions -> optionally add sparse segmented dKV epilogue before global writes. The first gate should measure the full sparse-attention backward boundary, not just late dKV.
decision.e2e_reason: This is still an isolated diagnostic route-grid kernel with materialized probability and grad-logit tensors. It is not wired into TorchTitan, so there is no materialized TFLOPs uplift to report yet.
